## Work Completed

### 1. Bridge v2 Deployment

**What was done:**
- Created `windows_bridge_v2.py` on Desktop (port 9091)
- Fixed window detection (hwnd parsing for 64-bit Windows)
- Fixed window focus (AttachThreadInput trick)
- Added self-restart endpoint

**Key technical details:**
```python
# Fixed: WNDENUMPROC callback for 64-bit Windows
WNDENUMPROC = ctypes.WINFUNCTYPE(
    ctypes.c_bool, ctypes.wintypes.HWND, ctypes.wintypes.LPARAM
)

# Fixed: Window focus with AttachThreadInput
user32.AttachThreadInput(our_thread_id, foreground_thread_id, True)
user32.AttachThreadInput(our_thread_id, target_thread_id, True)
user32.SetForegroundWindow(hwnd_val)
```

**Files created:**
- `C:\Users\ahols\Desktop\windows_bridge_v2.py`

**Testing:**
- ✅ Window detection finds "Ironcore Arena (DEBUG)"
- ✅ Screenshots capture correctly
- ✅ Click automation works
- ✅ Game launch/stop functional

---

### 2. Main Menu Button Fix

**Problem:** Buttons not responding to clicks in redesigned main menu

**Root Cause:** Godot 4 Control nodes default to `MOUSE_FILTER_STOP`, which eats clicks and doesn't pass them to children. Decorative nodes, grid pattern, and title labels were blocking button input.

**Solution Applied:**

1. Set decorative elements to `MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE`:
```gdscript
# main_menu_redesigned.gd
bg.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
grid.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
decorative_nodes.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
title_labels.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
```

2. Set button container to `MOUSE_FILTER_PASS`:
```gdscript
button_container.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_PASS
```

3. Set buttons to `MOUSE_FILTER_STOP`:
```gdscript
btn.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_STOP
```

**Files modified:**
- `src/ui/main_menu_redesigned.gd`
- `scenes/main_menu.tscn` (switched to redesigned script)
- `scenes/main.tscn` (scene references)

**Verification:**
- ✅ Bot Builder button works
- ✅ Arcade Mode button works
- ✅ All menu buttons functional

---

### 3. Main Menu Overlay Fix

**Problem:** Hidden main menu was still blocking input when builder screen was shown

**Root Cause:** `scene_flow_manager.gd` only set `visible = false` but the main menu retained default `MOUSE_FILTER_STOP`

**Solution:**
```gdscript
# scene_flow_manager.gd
func _hide_main_menu():
    main_menu.visible = false
    main_menu.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE  # Don't block input when hidden
```

**File modified:**
- `src/managers/scene_flow_manager.gd`

---

### 4. UIManager Conflict Resolution

**Problem:** Duplicate screen management systems conflicting

**Discovery:** Both `UIManager.gd` (autoload) and `SceneFlowManager` were trying to manage screens

**Solution:** Disabled UIManager autoload in project.godot:
```ini
[autoload]
; UIManager="*res://autoload/UIManager.gd"  ; DISABLED - conflicts with SceneFlowManager
```

**File modified:**
- `project.godot`

---

### 5. Builder Screen Investigation (IN PROGRESS)

**Problem:** Test Battle and Back buttons completely non-responsive

**Attempts made (chronological):**

| Attempt | Approach | Result |
|---------|----------|--------|
| 1 | Set parent containers to PASS | ❌ Failed |
| 2 | Set buttons to STOP | ❌ Failed |
| 3 | Debug signal connections | ❌ Signals connected but not firing |
| 4 | Button replacement (queue_free + create) | ❌ Failed |
| 5 | call_deferred replacement | ❌ Failed |
| 6 | top_level buttons at absolute positions | ❌ Failed |
| 7 | Rebuild with UIButton class | ❌ Failed |
| 8 | Root node + BottomBar mouse_filter PASS | ❌ Still testing |

**Claude Code Analysis:**
- Identified root cause: Godot 4 defaults to `MOUSE_FILTER_STOP` on Control nodes
- Root BuildScreen node was blocking all input
- BottomBar container was blocking button input

**Fixes applied:**
```gdscript
# build_screen.gd
func _setup_container_filters():
    self.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_PASS  # Was STOP
    $MarginContainer/VBox/BottomBar.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_PASS  # Was STOP
```

**Current status:** Awaiting test results after filter fixes

**Files modified:**
- `src/ui/build_screen.gd` (complete rebuild with UIButton)
- `scenes/build_screen.tscn` (cleaned up old button nodes)

---

## Lessons Learned

### 1. Godot 4 Mouse Filter Defaults
**Issue:** Control nodes default to `MOUSE_FILTER_STOP`, silently eating input

**Prevention:** Always explicitly set mouse_filter on:
- Root Control nodes → PASS or IGNORE
- Container nodes → PASS
- Interactive elements → STOP
- Decorative elements → IGNORE

### 2. Hidden UI Still Blocks Input
**Issue:** Setting `visible = false` doesn't prevent input blocking

**Prevention:** Always pair visibility changes with mouse_filter changes:
```gdscript
menu.visible = false
menu.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
```

### 3. Duplicate Autoload Conflicts
**Issue:** Multiple managers trying to control same systems

**Prevention:** Audit autoloads regularly; disable unused/conflicting singletons

### 4. Debugging Strategy
**What worked:**
- Systematic elimination (test main menu vs builder)
- Checking signal connections
- Verifying node instantiation
- Comparing working vs broken implementations

**What didn't work:**
- Assuming button class was the issue
- Multiple fixes at once (hard to isolate)
- Not checking root node filters early

---

## Prevention Checklist

For future UI work:

- [ ] Set root Control.mouse_filter explicitly
- [ ] Set all container.mouse_filter to PASS
- [ ] Set decorative elements to IGNORE
- [ ] Set interactive elements to STOP
- [ ] Test buttons immediately after creation
- [ ] Verify no duplicate managers/autoloads
- [ ] Document mouse_filter strategy in code comments
